channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Coronation, Alberta opens community-funded veterinary clinic to address rural livestock vet shortage
item.description: Rural Alberta is facing a critical shortage of veterinarians, particularly for large livestock, and the Town of Coronation along with Paintearth County has launched an innovative solution. Through the...

item.title: Trump attends White House Easter Egg Roll amid ongoing conflict in Iran
item.description: On 6 April 2026, the White House hosted its annual Easter Egg Roll, welcoming families and children to the South Lawn in a display of patriotic pageantry ahead of America's 250th birthday. President D...

item.title: Supreme Court to Hear NCERT Academics Barred over Judicial Corruption Chapter
item.description: Three academics involved in drafting a now-withdrawn NCERT textbook chapter on judicial corruption have approached the Supreme Court seeking a hearing after being barred from participating in governme...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal schools shine at Kearsney Easter Rugby Festival despite storm challenges
item.description: The 2026 Kearsney Easter Rugby Festival wrapped up in Botha’s Hill on Monday, marking a triumphant weekend for KwaZulu-Natal’s premier school sides. Durban High School (DHS), Westville, and hosts Kear...

item.title: Community and Celebrities Support Mother Moving into Incomplete House
item.description: Joy Asiwaju, a mother of three, went viral after moving into her unfinished three-bedroom house with her children. Despite lacking basic amenities like toilets and proper flooring, she shared a house ...

item.title: The Importance of Learning to Say No and Set Boundaries
item.description: Saying no is a crucial life skill that many people struggle to practice. Social norms, cultural expectations, and generational influences often make it difficult to decline requests without feeling gu...

item.title: Israel-Iran Tensions Escalate as Intelligence Chief Dies in Strike Amid Gulf Crisis
item.description: The war between Israel and Iran don dey reach dangerous levels as Israeli strikes kill Majid Khademi, wey be the intelligence chief of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards. This attack happen early Monday, sho...

item.title: Microsoft Defends Copilot's Disclaimer Amid Industry-Wide AI Reliability Concerns
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot tool is drawing widespread attention after users discovered a clause in its terms of service stating that Copilot is 'for entertainment purposes only.' The wording, last updated on...

item.title: Legacy of Solidarity: Anglican Leader Resigns Over Gaza War, Following Family's Defiance
item.description: A prominent Anglican leader in Australia has resigned from their position with the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade in protest against the ongoing war in Gaza. This decision is deeply rooted in...

item.title: PM Modi urges voters to reject Congress for peace and development in Assam
item.description: On April 6, 2026, during his campaign for Assam Assembly elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi called on voters to keep the Congress away from power, stressing that only by doing so could the state ...

item.title: Quezon City Strengthens Rabies Vaccination Efforts to Eliminate Deaths by 2030
item.description: The Quezon City government has ramped up its initiatives to eliminate rabies-related deaths by 2030, emphasizing the importance of pet vaccination. Last year, over 211,000 residents and more than 210,...

item.title: Maruhubi Terminal shows strong performance in operational test ahead of full opening
item.description: Zanzibar is making solid progress towards the full commissioning of the new Maruhubi Passenger and Roll-on/Roll-off (RoRo) Terminal after a successful operational test conducted by ZF DevCo. The test,...

item.title: Trump criticises Starmer over UK's stance on Iran conflict
item.description: During a White House press briefing, former US President Donald Trump criticised UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer for not fully supporting US military actions in Iran. Trump likened Starmer's approa...

item.title: Sask. Human Rights Commission to Reconsider Complaint Over Language Restrictions at Hospital Starbucks Kiosk
item.description: A Court of King's Bench judge in Saskatchewan has instructed the Saskatchewan Human Rights Commission (SHRC) to reconsider a complaint previously dismissed regarding a language policy at a Starbucks k...

item.title: Netanyahu Removes Aide Following Allegations of Racist Remarks
item.description: Well, seems like things are getting a bit messy over there in Israel. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has had to show his top aide the door after some pretty serious allegations popped up. Apparentl...

item.title: Zoos Provide Critical Antivenom for Exotic Snake Bites in the U.S.
item.description: The article highlights the dangers faced by American exotic snake owners and the critical role of zoos in providing antivenom. Chris Gifford, a North Carolina snake keeper, nearly died after a green m...

item.title: Government introduces e-Prescription system to improve medicine supply and management
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has launched a new initiative to deal with the continuing shortage of medicines in health facilities by introducing an electronic prescribing and dispensing system, known as...

item.title: Apple Seeks Supreme Court Review Over External Payment Fees in Epic Games Case
item.description: Apple is preparing to appeal to the U.S. Supreme Court over its long-running legal dispute with Epic Games concerning App Store fees. The case originated when Epic introduced external payment options ...

item.title: How the manosphere is growing through misogyny, money and online influence
item.description: The article looks at the growing influence of the manosphere, an online ecosystem made up of male-focused spaces, influencers and communities that often promote anti-women ideas, rigid gender roles an...

item.title: Celebrating Gbenga Daniel's 70th Birthday: A Legacy of Governance and Entrepreneurship
item.description: Gbenga Daniel, former governor of Ogun State and now Senator, is celebrated for his dual contributions in both business and politics. Starting off as a successful entrepreneur with his Kresta Laurel b...
